data.text: TREASURIES-US bonds moderately higher on safe-haven buying fueled by Iran fears
data.snippet: U.S. Treasury prices edged higher on Thursday on the back of some safe-haven bids as investors grew nervous about tensions involving Iran, also pricing in expectations that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady at least for the first half of the year.

image.text: A bronze seal for the Department of the Treasury is shown at the U.S. Treasury building in Washington, U.S., January 20, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: CANADA FX DEBT-Canadian dollar edges lower on flight to safety
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Canadian dollar edged lower against its U.S. counterpart on Thursday, as safe-haven demand for the greenback offset domestic data that showed a sharp narrowing in Canada's current account deficit.

data.text: Euro zone yields hold near multi-month lows; inflation data awaited
data.snippet: Euro zone government bond yields steadied near multi-month lows after Nvidia’s results beat expectations, supporting risk appetite, with investors now awaiting inflation data from Germany, France and Spain on Friday.

image.text: Euro and U.S. dollar banknotes are seen in this illustration taken May 4, 2025.

data.text: US mortgage rates dip below 6%, but supply remains key
data.snippet: The average rate on the popular U.S. 30-year fixed-rate mortgage fell below 6% this week for the first time in 3-1/2 years, but economists said the improvement was likely temporary and on its own insufficient to boost housing demand unless supply increased.

image.text: U.S. dollar banknotes are seen in this illustration taken May 4, 2025.

data.text: Japan's shorter-end yields rise as hawkish BOJ comments fan early rate hike bets
data.snippet: Japan's shorter-dated bond yields rose on Thursday, reversing the previous session's declines, as hawkish comments from the Bank of Japan officials stoked bets of early interest rate hikes.

image.text: Banknotes of Japanese yen are seen in this illustration picture taken September 22, 2022.

data.text: INDIA BONDS-Indian bonds fall ahead of debt auction, growth data
data.snippet: Indian government bonds ended lower on Thursday after a choppy session, as traders looked ahead to the penultimate debt auction of the current financial year and the first GDP growth data under a new series.

image.text: A vendor displays various denominations of Indian currency at a roadside currency exchange stall in the old quarters of Delhi, India, February 2, 2026.

data.text: University of California sells $2 billion in debt while facing Trump crackdown
data.snippet: The University of California sold about $2 billion worth of general revenue bonds in the municipal market on Wednesday while U.S. President Donald Trump attempts a crackdown on the educational institution and other top schools.

image.text: U.S. dollar banknotes are seen in this illustration taken May 4, 2025

data.text: Indonesia raises $4.54 billion in yuan and euro bonds auction
data.snippet: Indonesia has raised 9.25 billion yuan ($1.35 billion) and 2.7 billion euros ($3.19 billion) from an auction of yuan and euro denominated bonds, its finance ministry said on Thursday.

image.text: A general view of the city skyline of Jakarta, the capital city of Indonesia, October 30, 2021.

data.text: Abu Dhabi raises $3 billion from two-tranche bond sale
data.snippet: Abu Dhabi on Thursday raised a total of $3 billion from a two-tranche U.S. dollar-denominated bonds sale, fixed income news service IFR reported, in its first issuance this year.

relatedHeadlines.text: UAE bonds to be phased out of JPMorgan EM indices due to rising wealth
relatedHeadlines.snippet: United Arab Emirates bonds are set to be removed from JPMorgan's closely tracked Emerging Markets Sovereign Bond Indices as a result of its increasing income levels, the bank said on Monday, adding that Czech Republic's local debt could also be removed.

data.text: Australian dollar bond market to see record issuance, says NAB chief
data.snippet: Australia's growing pension system is helping fuel record issuance in the A$ bond market this year, a trend National Australia Bank (NAB) CEO Andrew Irvine expects to be maintained as global borrowers tap deepening pools of domestic liquidity.

image.text: Australian dollars are seen in an illustration photo February 8, 2018

data.text: Singapore's OCBC prices $500 million subordinated notes at 4.517%
data.snippet: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp (OCBC) on Thursday priced $500 million of tier 2 fixed rate subordinated notes under its $30 billion global medium-term note programme at 4.517%.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Singapore's OCBC bank sees 2026 income stable to rising in cautious outlook
